mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate relative to recent inflation readings implies easier policy and lower expected real rates, which compresses the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when FEDFUNDS is already stable or rising while CPIAUCSL acceleration persists.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is flat or down versus its prior 3-month average and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is not accelerating
failure_condition: If triggered instances show positive 20d Treasury yield changes in more than 60% of cases, or if CPIAUCSL acceleration offsets policy easing and the yield does not fall, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand slowdown signaled by weaker RSAFS and softer GDPC1 indicates lower nominal growth expectations, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when retail sales weaken but inflation or labor data simultaneously re-accelerate.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d yield changes are not negative on average during demand slowdowns, or if the sign reverses in periods where GDP and retail sales weaken together.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE above its recent range signals labor-market slack and weaker wage pressure, reducing rate-hike expectations and lowering the 10-year Treasury yield within 20 trading days. The hypothesis fails if unemployment rises without any concurrent moderation in inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 3-month low
failure_condition: If yield declines are not concentrated after the UNRATE threshold is crossed, or if the average 20d yield change is positive in those episodes, reject the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly INDPRO growth combined with elevated HOUST suggests resilient real activity and construction demand, which raises near-term growth and term-premium expectations, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when industrial output improves but housing activity remains weak.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while HOUST is above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO-HOUST strength does not correspond to positive 20d yield changes, or if the effect disappears when one leg of the interaction is absent.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation momentum, the market prices a delayed but tighter policy path, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days. The hypothesis fails if inflation rises but rate expectations do not reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: If short-horizon yield changes are not positive after inflation acceleration episodes, or if the relationship flips negative when FEDFUNDS is already restrictive, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates weakening household income and spending, which lowers expected nominal growth and compresses the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when payroll weakness is offset by still-strong sales growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines from the prior month and RSAFS growth is below zero
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-sales weakness does not lead to negative 20d yield changes, or if yield declines occur without the joint weakness condition.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market deterioration that is visible only when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS contracts in the same month should depress the 10-year Treasury yield by signaling weaker income growth and lower policy pressure. The effect fails if unemployment rises but payrolls continue to expand strongly.
failure_condition: If the joint deterioration does not produce negative forward yield changes over a 20d horizon, or if the interaction is dominated by single-series effects, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO together with a bottoming HOUST series signals re-acceleration in real activity and stronger intermediate-term inflation pressure, which should push the 10-year Treasury yield higher over a 60-day horizon. The mechanism fails when output improves but housing remains in contraction.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month trend turns positive and HOUST stops falling on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward yield changes are not positive following the joint recovery pattern, or if the signal reverses when housing remains weak despite rising industrial production.
